title: Paul Weller: The Modfather Embraces Family and New Music
summary: Paul Weller, the legendary frontman of The Jam and a key figure in the mod revival, is set to release a new album titled 'Weller At The BBC Volume 2'. The album features live performances of his classic hits and covers of other artists' songs, including Billie Eilish's 'What Was I Made For?' from the Barbie movie soundtrack. Weller, now 67, says he was inspired to cover the song because his youngest daughter loves it. He praises Eilish's songwriting, calling the melody beautiful and the lyrics clever. Weller has also quit drinking, which he says has made him more appreciative of music. Reflecting on his early career, he recalls his first gig at Woking Working Men's Club, where only 10 people attended, most of whom complained about the noise until after bingo. Today, he has a studio in Ripley where he creates music. Weller has two grandchildren and downplays being a 'cool granddad', instead saying he is just like any other grandparent. He enjoys sharing music with his eight children, but emphasizes that the exchange is mutual.

background_read: Paul Weller, born in 1958, is an English singer-songwriter and musician. He gained fame as the lead vocalist and guitarist of The Jam (1972-1982), a band that was central to the mod revival. He later formed The Style Council and has had a successful solo career. Known as 'The Modfather', he has influenced many artists. He has eight children from various relationships.
Article_Structure: Main Points: Paul Weller releases new live album, covers Billie Eilish, reflects on family and early career. Purpose: To promote his album and share personal insights. Evidence Evaluation: Article relies on Weller's own statements, which are firsthand but subjective. Author Credibility: BBC is a reputable source. Methodology: Information gathered from an interview with Weller. Critical Assessment: The article provides a personal perspective but lacks independent analysis.

perspectives.description: He feels lucky to be a granddad and enjoys making music with family influence.
Fans appreciate his continued creativity and personal growth.
